mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 320 in the CPIAUCSL series signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL > 320, or inflation declines below 315 without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signals robust supply-side dynamics, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of concurrent INDPRO/GDPC1 growth fall below 5% annualized, or the correlation between INDPRO and GDPC1 drops below 0.7.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a demand-side imbalance, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reassess sectoral risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST < 1300 and RSAFS > 720000, or the HOUST/RSAFS spread narrows by >15% without equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) paired with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals tightening labor markets, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through elevated corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS > 150000 and UNRATE < 4.2, or the PAYEMS/UNRATE correlation exceeds 0.85 without equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) without corresponding retail sales (RSAFS) growth indicates overbuilding in the construction sector, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through sectoral overvaluation.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST > 1350 and RSAFS < 725000, or the HOUST/RSAFS spread widens by >20% without equity losses.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a hawkish central bank, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through increased equity discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 4.2 and CPIAUCSL > 315, or the FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL spread narrows by >0.5% without equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate weakening supply-side capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate profitability expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed 10% annualized in periods of consecutive INDPRO declines, or INDPRO reverses its downward trend without corresponding equity gains.
